easy.title: Sentence Transformation: FCE-style (guided)
easy.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between two and five words (FCE Part 4).

items.sentence1: I last saw him in May.
items.explanation: Present perfect: haven't seen (experience since a point in time).

items.sentence1: It's possible that she forgot the meeting.
items.explanation: Modal of possibility in the past: might have + past participle.

items.sentence1: They are building a new school in the town.
items.explanation: Passive present continuous: is being built.

items.sentence1: I don't have enough money to buy a car.
We couldn't go out because of the rain.
She started working here three years ago.

easy.title: Sentence Transformation: FCE-style (2)
easy.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between two and five words (FCE Part 4).

items.sentence1: The film was so boring that we left early.
items.sentence2_suffix: a boring film that we left early.

items.sentence1: I can't wait to see you.
He didn't go to work because he was ill.
Perhaps she missed the bus.
items.explanation: Might have + past participle (possibility in the past).

items.sentence1: They are repairing the road.
It's possible that he didn't hear the alarm.

easy.title: Sentence Transformation: FCE-style (3)
easy.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between two and five words (FCE Part 4).

items.sentence1: I haven't been to the cinema for months.
items.explanation: Since the last time + past simple.

items.sentence1: She doesn't have enough time to help us.
We couldn't find the keys anywhere.
We are getting someone to paint the house.
items.explanation: Have something done: we are having the house painted.

medium.title: Sentence Transformation: FCE Part 4
medium.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between two and five words.

items.sentence1: They cancelled the match because of the weather.
I regret not studying harder for the exam.
items.explanation: I wish + past perfect (regret about the past).

items.sentence1: Nobody in the class is taller than Maria.
It wasn't necessary for you to bring a gift.
items.explanation: Needn't have + past participle = it wasn't necessary (but you did).

items.sentence1: They say that the painting is worth millions.
items.explanation: Passive reporting: is said to be.

items.sentence1: I've never been to Japan before.
items.explanation: It's the first time + present perfect.

medium.title: Sentence Transformation: FCE Part 4 (2)
medium.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between two and five words.

items.sentence1: She doesn't allow her children to watch TV after 9 pm.
items.sentence2_suffix: her children watch TV after 9 pm.

items.sentence1: I'm sure he will pass the exam.
They didn't let us enter the building.
It isn't necessary to book in advance.
The accident happened because the driver was tired.
She has never been to Paris before.
items.explanation: It's the first time + present perfect.

medium.title: Sentence Transformation: FCE Part 4 (3)
medium.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between two and five words.

items.sentence1: We couldn't go swimming because the sea was too rough.
I last ate sushi a year ago.
Perhaps they got lost.
This is the best pizza I've ever tasted.
They say he is very rich.
You were wrong to shout at her.

hard.title: Sentence Transformation: CAE Part 4
hard.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between three and six words.

items.sentence1: I had no idea that he was leaving.
items.explanation: Unaware of the fact that + clause.

items.sentence1: She was the only person who could solve the problem.
They didn't succeed in finding a solution.
It's not worth trying to change his mind.
The manager said we could leave early.
I don't object to you using my phone.

hard.title: Sentence Transformation: CAE Part 4 (2)
hard.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between three and six words.

items.sentence1: I don't think she will agree.
They didn't manage to finish the project on time.
It's not worth complaining about it.
She was the only person who knew the answer.
He didn't realise the danger until it was too late.
items.sentence2_prefix: Only when it was too late

items.sentence1: We were not allowed to take photos.

hard.title: Sentence Transformation: CAE Part 4 (3)
hard.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between three and six words.

items.sentence1: I had no idea she was his sister.
They insisted that we should stay for dinner.
The problem was too complex for us to solve.
She has no intention of leaving.
We couldn't find a solution to the problem.
He continued to work despite his illness.

expert.title: Sentence Transformation: CPE Part 4
expert.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between three and eight words.

items.sentence1: Under no circumstances should you leave the building.
items.explanation: On no account = under no circumstances (inversion).

items.sentence1: It was only when I got home that I realised my mistake.
items.explanation: Inversion after 'only when': did I realise.

items.sentence1: She is unlikely to agree to the proposal.
They had no intention of apologising.
items.explanation: Had no intention of + -ing / did not intend to + infinitive.

items.sentence1: The outcome was not what we had expected.
He was determined to succeed despite the setbacks.
items.explanation: Such + be + noun + that (inversion for emphasis).

expert.title: Sentence Transformation: CPE Part 4 (2)
expert.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between three and eight words.

items.sentence1: Nobody could have predicted the outcome.
She was so determined that nothing could stop her.
They didn't take my advice.
items.explanation: Take heed of / pay heed to.

items.sentence1: It was only when I read the report that I understood the scale.
items.sentence2_suffix: the report that I understood the scale.

items.sentence1: The results did not meet our expectations.
There is little chance that she will change her mind.

expert.title: Sentence Transformation: CPE Part 4 (3)
expert.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word in capitals. Do not change the word. You must use between three and eight words.

items.sentence1: You must not leave the room under any circumstances.
It was only when she apologized that I forgave her.
They are unlikely to reach an agreement.
He was determined to win despite the odds.
The plan did not live up to our expectations.
I had no idea that they were related.
